title: Harry Potter and the Philosopher's Stone
author: J.K. Rowling
description: A young boy discovers he is a wizard and begins his journey at Hogwarts School of Witchcraft and Wizardry.

title: The Lord of the Rings
author: J.R.R. Tolkien
description: An epic fantasy adventure following Frodo Baggins on a quest to destroy the One Ring and save Middle-earth.

title: Brave New World
author: Aldous Huxley
description: A dystopian novel set in a futuristic society where people are genetically engineered and conditioned for control and stability.

title: The Alchemist
author: Paulo Coelho
description: A philosophical novel about a young Andalusian shepherd's journey to find treasure and discover his personal legend.

title: The Pragmatic Programmer
author: Andrew Hunt & David Thomas
description: A timeless guide covering programming best practices, tools, and the mindset needed to become an effective software developer.

title: Sapiens
author: Yuval Noah Harari
description: A sweeping narrative of humanity's history from the Stone Age through the 21st century, blending history and science.

author: George Orwell
description: A dystopian novel set in a totalitarian society where Big Brother surveils every citizen and truth is controlled by the state.

title: You Don't Know JS
author: Kyle Simpson
description: A deep dive into the core mechanisms of the JavaScript language, covering types, scopes, closures, and async patterns.

title: The Gene: An Intimate History
author: Siddhartha Mukherjee
description: A compelling history of the gene — from Mendel's peas to CRISPR — and its profound impact on identity, medicine, and society.

title: To Kill a Mockingbird
author: Harper Lee
description: A powerful story of racial injustice and moral growth seen through the eyes of young Scout Finch in the American South.

title: Designing Data-Intensive Applications
author: Martin Kleppmann
description: An in-depth guide to the principles behind building scalable, reliable, and maintainable data systems and distributed databases.

title: The Selfish Gene
author: Richard Dawkins
description: A groundbreaking work in evolutionary biology that introduces the gene-centered view of evolution and the concept of memes.
